requirement: Only one daemon may own a data directory while distinct directories remain independent.

evidence.test: a second lock for the same data directory reports a clear conflict
different data directories can be locked at the same time

requirement: Migrations run only in a fixed forward order and reject databases newer than the program.

evidence.test: empty database applies migrations in order and records complete metadata
database newer than program is rejected before any pending migration runs

requirement: Applied migration checksum drift prevents startup before pending mutations.

evidence.test: checksum drift is rejected before any pending migration runs

requirement: A failed migration restores a verified consistent backup and quarantines failed bytes.

evidence.test: bootstrap closes, quarantines, and restores the pre-migration backup after a later migration fails

requirement: Empty, 1.x, and uncheckpointed-WAL fixtures complete backup, migration, Provider, transaction, clock, shutdown, and restart lifecycles.

evidence.test: Phase 0A lifecycle adopts 1.x, transacts with database time, closes, and restarts
createMigrationBackup includes uncheckpointed WAL commits and writes a verified manifest
empty scenario performs the full bootstrap while schema version zero stays unmanaged
Phase 0A empty database transacts with fixed time, closes, and restarts
factory preserves a real uncheckpointed WAL fixture until cleanup
Phase 0A factory closes the WAL migration loop and restarts under one instance lock

requirement: Production business database access is owned by DatabaseProvider and test-only factories stay out of public production exports.

evidence.test: production SQLite ownership and transaction control stay inside infrastructure
production build verifier rejects test-only Provider artifacts

requirement: Business writes use synchronous Transaction callbacks without externally awaitable side effects.

evidence.test: business writes use synchronous transaction callbacks and database time
nested transactions and Promise callbacks are rejected and rolled back

requirement: Persisted generated timestamps come from the injected Database Clock.

evidence.test: generated persistence timestamps use one injected database Clock value

requirement: Every Provider connection applies and verifies the role-specific SQLite policy, including WAL and foreign keys.

evidence.test: uses the migrated schema and applies production SQLite policy before reads
restart exposes equivalent frozen diagnostics
snapshot policy securely accepts bounded WAL and rollback snapshots

requirement: SQLITE_BUSY retry is bounded and never replays an executed business callback.

evidence.test: real BEGIN busy never calls the callback and retries once after the competing connection releases
COMMIT busy rolls back without replaying an already executed callback
retry budget accepts exactly 2000ms and rejects one millisecond more before BEGIN

requirement: Shutdown drains operations, checkpoints, closes connections, releases Instance Lock, and removes owned test files.

evidence.test: application initialization failure closes Provider and releases the real lock
factory closes in parallel, releases locks, and removes directories
factory close is idempotent and returns one cleanup promise

requirement: With the 2.0 feature flag disabled, startup performs no 2.0 schema creation or business dual-write.

evidence.test: feature flag off completes a database lifecycle without 2.0 schema or business writes

requirement: Published 1.x schema and representative historical data remain readable and byte-preserving through baseline adoption.

evidence.test: migration bootstrap adopts an exact 1.x fixture without replaying baseline DDL
current SessionStore reads the normal 1.x fixture after baseline adoption
legacy fixture historical variants are deterministic and preserve known anomalies
